title: Using a Green Cone food digester for cooked food and meat
subtitle: How an in-ground digester handles food waste that a standard compost heap cannot
excerpt: A Green Cone is a double-walled cone mounted over a basket buried in the ground. It accepts all food waste including cooked food, meat, fish, and dairy that cannot go in a standard compost heap. Solar heating accelerates decomposition; waste is absorbed into the surrounding soil rather than collected as finished compost.
sourceNotes: Green Cone UK product guidance and installation instructions. WRAP guidance on in-vessel and in-ground food digesters. Local authority subsidy programme guidance for food waste digesters.

content.text: solves the problem that most home composters encounter: what to do with cooked food, meat scraps, fish, and dairy. These materials attract rats and other pests in a standard open compost heap. A Green Cone buries the waste in a basket below ground, where soil microorganisms and worms break it down. The cone above ground acts as a solar collector and allows gas exchange, making conditions favourable for aerobic decomposition.

Choose a sunny position to maximise solar heating. Dig a hole approximately 450 mm deep and 450 mm wide. Place the basket in the hole and backfill soil around it. Insert the double-walled cone over the basket. The cone should be oriented to receive maximum sun (south-facing if possible). Add the accelerator powder (supplied) with the first addition of food waste to introduce the microbial culture.
All fruit and vegetable waste, including citrus and onion
Cooked food: leftover rice, pasta, bread, cooked vegetables
Meat and fish scraps, including bones (bones decompose slowly but are not harmful)

A single Green Cone handles the food waste of a household of 2-4 people. Decomposition rate slows in cold weather: in winter, the basket fills faster than it empties. Sprinkling accelerator powder each time you add waste maintains microbial activity. If the basket becomes full, lift the cone and basket, move to a new position, and allow the original site to fully absorb the remaining material over the following months while the new site begins accepting fresh waste.

Some local authorities subsidise the cost of a Green Cone or similar in-ground digester as part of their household food waste reduction programmes. Check your council website before buying at full price.
